name: 2Africa
owner: Meta Platforms, MTN, Orange, Telecom Egypt, Vodafone (21-member consortium)
notes: World's longest subsea cable. Encircles entire African continent.

name: SEA-ME-WE 5
owner: Consortium: Orange, SingTel, Tata Communications, Telecom Italia, NTT, Telstra
notes: South East Asia - Middle East - Western Europe route.

name: SEA-ME-WE 6
owner: Consortium: Singtel, China Mobile, China Telecom, Bharti Airtel, Orange, Telin
notes: Next-generation successor to SEA-ME-WE 5. Expected capacity: 100+ Tbps.

name: AAE-1
owner: Consortium: du, Ooredoo, STC, Tata, Vodafone, China Unicom
notes: Asia Africa Europe 1. One of the longest cables connecting Asia to Europe.

name: MAREA
owner: Microsoft + Meta (Facebook)
notes: First dedicated subsea cable owned by tech hyperscalers. Capacity: 200 Tbps.

name: FASTER
owner: Google, China Mobile International, Global Transit, KDDI, SingTel, PLDT
notes: Trans-Pacific cable. Google's first major subsea investment. Capacity: 60 Tbps.

name: Dunant
owner: Google
notes: Google's private trans-Atlantic cable. Named after Red Cross founder Henri Dunant. Capacity: 250 Tbps.

name: Equiano
owner: Google
notes: Google's cable connecting Europe to Africa. Named after Olaudah Equiano.

name: JUPITER
owner: Google, Meta (Facebook), PCCW Global, SoftBank, PLDT, Amazon
notes: Trans-Pacific cable. Largest subsea cable to Philippines. Capacity: 60 Tbps.

name: India Europe Xpress (IEX)
owner: Reliance Jio (Jio Platforms)
notes: India's first privately owned international subsea cable.

name: Tata TGN-Eurasia
owner: Tata Communications
notes: Tata's flagship international cable linking UK to East Asia.

name: FLAG Europe Asia (FEA)
owner: GCX (Global Cloud Xchange) — formerly Reliance Globalcom
notes: One of the longest cables in operation. FLAG = Fiber-optic Link Around the Globe.

name: SEACOM
owner: SEACOM Ltd (African-owned consortium)
notes: First privately-owned cable serving East Africa. Critical for African internet access.

name: EASSy
owner: Consortium: Bharti Airtel, MTN, Vodacom, Telkom South Africa, and others
notes: Eastern Africa Submarine System. Connects East Africa coast from Sudan to South Africa.

name: Bay of Bengal Gateway (BBG)
owner: Consortium: Bharti Airtel, Tata Communications, SingTel, Telekom Malaysia
notes: Serves Bay of Bengal region. Critical connectivity for South Asia.

name: PEACE Cable
owner: PEACE Cable International Network (Pakistan, China, Europe consortium)
notes: Pakistan-East Africa-Indian Ocean-Europe Cable. China-funded infrastructure.

name: SEA-ME-WE 4 (SMW4)
owner: Consortium: France Telecom, SingTel, Bharti Airtel, Telecom Pakistan, and others
notes: South East Asia - Middle East - Western Europe 4. Backbone of internet across Indian Ocean.

name: SAFE
owner: Consortium: Telkom South Africa, France Telecom, Bharti Airtel, Tata
notes: South Africa Far East cable. Links southern Africa to Asia.

name: ACE (Africa Coast to Europe)
owner: Orange (lead), Mauritius Telecom, Sonatel, Maroc Telecom, and others
notes: Connects West Africa from France to South Africa. 24 landing points total.

name: SJC (South-East Asia Japan Cable)
owner: Google, Bharti Airtel, KDDI, SingTel, PLDT, SoftBank, Global Transit
notes: Connects Japan to South East Asia. Google's first Asian subsea cable investment.
